title: Fractions in contexts
description: Practice identifying fractions in real world contexts.

assessment_items.question_content:
The math club has $4$ members. $1$ of the members is $8$ years old.

**What fraction of the math club is $8$ years old?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\purpleD4$ students in the math club.

![A row of 4 dots.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f41d33a3039c5a0ed50b4abf0da932bb9df9e8c6)

$\blueD1$ of the students is $8$ years old.

![The same with 1 of the 4 dots highlighted.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/58c1f44a7024fb5f6b048042b07a11c5edfb9e10)
$\blueD1\purpleD{\text{ fourth}}$ of the math club is $8$ years old.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $2$ apples in Sophie's lunch box. $1$ of the apples is rotten.

**What fraction of apples are rotten?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\redD2$ apples.

![A row of 2 dots.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/391322f0e7627b053e051eb41a7ccc971c5c7d73)

$\blueD1$ of the apples is rotten.

![The same with 1 of the 2 dots highlighted.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f25bcd7a1893fc56af763b94bf7165971727903)
$\dfrac{\blueD1}{\redD{2}}$ of the apples are rotten.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $3$ balls outside during recess. $2$ of the balls are soccer balls.

**What fraction of balls are soccer balls?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\greenE3$ balls.

![A row of 3 dots.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/475d3b440e21f3b5ad2c063496448f09d49f8611)

$\goldD2$ of the balls are soccer balls.

![The same with 2 of the 3 dots highlighted.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ab1f5e206d806e64530ab092423e4ac7d3bee02)
$\goldD2\greenD{\text{ thirds}}$ of the balls are soccer balls.

assessment_items.question_content:
On the Sharks dive team, there are $3$ divers in third grade.  There are $8$ total divers on the team.

**What fraction of Sharks dive team is in third grade?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\tealD8$ divers on the Sharks dive team.

![A row of 8 dots.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ae88fc8d1d8dad5e25bdcc5c6fd24492d074a46e)

$\goldD3$ of the divers are in third grade.

![The same with 3 of the 8 dots highlighted.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b5078b2fd2d92b6020c46210c574da356485853)
$\goldD3\tealD{\text{ eighths}}$ of the divers are in third grade.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mrs. Jones ordered $5$ cheese pizzas and $1$ pepperoni pizza.
She ordered a total of $6$ pizzas. 

**What fraction of pizzas are cheese?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\purpleC6$ pizzas.

![A row of 6 dots.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ba70ccfb8ebb6d1d995ed713ade0e8c40eda21c)

$\redD5$ of the pizzas are cheese.

![The same with 5 of the 6 dots highlighted.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b13610268c5418ff88f07b74b62575a98c052e8)
$\dfrac{\redD5}{\purpleC{6}}$ of the pizzas are cheese.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jeff has $7$ blue balloons. He has $8$ total balloons.

**What fraction of balloons are blue?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\greenD8$ balloons.

![A row of 8 dots.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2a28eec2a59bf313e318100c9d95ed38aca28d12)

$\blueD7$ of the balloons are blue.

![The same with 7 of the 8 dots highlighted.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce07b55ca507bf2e211cb7e77c94d1c0009fccad)
$\dfrac{\blueD7}{\greenD{8}}$ of the balloons are blue.

assessment_items.question_content:
Yuma divided his clay into $4$ equal parts.  He made clay animals out of $3$ of the parts.  

**What fraction of the clay did Yuma use to make clay animals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Yuma divided his clay into $\blueD4$ equal parts.


[[☃ image 1]]

Yuma used $\goldD3$ of the parts to make clay animals.


[[☃ image 1]]

Yuma used $\dfrac{\goldD3}{\blueD4}$ of the clay make clay animals.

assessment_items.question_content:
Milo cut his cake into $2$ equal pieces. He ate $2$ pieces of cake for his birthday.

**What fraction of the cake did Milo eat?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Milo divided his cake into $\blueD2$ equal parts.


[[☃ image 1]]

Milo ate $\goldD2$ pieces of cake.



[[☃ image 1]]

Milo ate $\dfrac{\goldD2}{\blueD2}$ of his cake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Beth ate $6$ pieces of a chocolate bar. The bar was split into $8$ equal size pieces. 

**What fraction of the chocolate bar did Beth eat?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Beth divided the chocolate bar into $\blueD8$ equal pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

Beth ate $\goldD6$ pieces of chocolate bar.


[[☃ image 1]]

Beth ate $\dfrac{\goldD6}{\blueD8}$ of her chocolate bar.

assessment_items.question_content:
Juanita cut her cheese into $4$ equal pieces. She gave $2$ pieces to her brother.

**What fraction of the cheese did Juanita give to her brother?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Juanita cut her cheese into $\blueD4$ equal pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

Juanita gave her brother $\goldD2$ pieces of cheese.


[[☃ image 1]]

Juanita gave ${\goldD{2}}{\blueD{\text{ fourths}}}$ of her cheese to her brother.

assessment_items.question_content:
Brian ate $5$ pieces of pie. The pie was cut into $6$ equal size pieces.

**What fraction of the pie did Brian eat?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The pie was cut into $\blueD6$ equal pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]

Brian ate $\goldD5$ pieces of pie.


[[☃ image 1]]

Brian ate ${\goldD{5}}{\blueD{\text{ sixths}}}$ of the pie.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sandra cut her sandwich into $3$ equal parts. She ate $1$ part for lunch.

**What fraction of the sandwich did Sandra eat for lunch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Sandra cut her sandwich into $\blueD3$ equal pieces.


[[☃ image 1]]


Sandra ate $\goldD1$ piece for lunch.


[[☃ image 1]]

Sandra ate ${\goldD{1}}{\blueD{\text{ third}}}$ of the sandwich for lunch.
